record.field: Trilium Notes is a self-hosted, hierarchical note-taking application for building large, structured personal knowledge bases.

Trilium Notes is a self-hosted, hierarchical note-taking application for building large, structured personal knowledge bases.

Trilium organizes information into an arbitrarily deep tree where a single note can be cloned into multiple locations at once. It combines a rich WYSIWYG editor with code editing, full-text search, attributes, scripting and a REST API, making it suited to power users who outgrow flat note apps. Originally created by zadam and now maintained by the community-led TriliumNext team, it runs as a desktop app, a self-hosted server, or both with synchronization.

Key features
  • Hierarchical notes with cloning, so one note can live in multiple places in the tree
  • Rich WYSIWYG editor plus source-code notes with syntax highlighting, tables, images and math
  • Fast full-text search, note navigation and per-note version history
  • Attributes, scripting and a REST API for automation and custom workflows
  • Visualization tools: relation/link maps, mind maps, canvas sketching and geo maps
  • Per-note encryption, OIDC/OpenID and TOTP authentication, and self-hosted sync
Best for

Individuals and technical users building a lifelong personal knowledge base, second brain or structured documentation who want full data ownership through self-hosting and advanced organization beyond a simple notes app.

services: trilium: image: zadam/trilium:latest ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:8080' restart: always volumes: - /root/trilium-backups:/home/node/trilium-data/backup container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true
